Summary: MyoD1 is an important transcription factor and co-activator of genes responsible for myocyte differentiation. In TAFAZZIN-knockout (TAZ-KO) myoblasts, decreased Wnt signaling is likely responsible for upregulation of Mohawk (MKX). MKX directly represses MyoD1 transcription. In TAFAZZIN-deficient cells, noncanonical Wnt signaling is implicated in dysfunctional MKX–MyoD1 regulation. MyoD1 expression is reported to depend on noncanonical rather than canonical Wnt signaling. Decreased MyoD1 in TAZ-KO myoblasts is associated with reduced myoblast differentiation and myogenesis defects in response to myogenic stimuli. Decreased MyoD1 in TAFAZZIN-deficient myoblasts is proposed to underlie skeletal myopathy in Barth syndrome. Loss of MyoD1 results in muscle regeneration defects in a Duchenne muscular dystrophy mouse model.
References: Vo, L., Schmidtke, M. W., Da Rosa-Junior, N. T., Ren, M., Schlame, M., & Greenberg, M. L. (2023). Cardiolipin metabolism regulates expression of muscle transcription factor MyoD1 and muscle development. J Biol Chem, 299(3), 102978. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jbc.2023.102978
